headline: Tense, release, and let the body settle
subtext: One muscle group at a time, feet to face.
steps: Starting at your feet, tense one muscle group for five seconds.
Release it suddenly and notice the wave of softening.
Work slowly up the body, breathing easily as you go.
caption:
Tension is a habit the body holds without asking. You can teach it to let go.

Progressive muscle relaxation works through the body one group at a time. You tense a muscle for a few seconds, then release, and feel the difference. Done slowly, it moves the nervous system from guarding to settling.

For people with chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus or long COVID, that shift matters. A calmer body is a quieter signal to an overprotective brain.

Five minutes before bed is a good place to start. More activities at https://neurowaze.health

#ChronicPain #NervousSystem #Relaxation #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Progressive muscle relaxation is a long-established way to down-regulate the body's stress response. Find guided activities and exercises in the Neurowaze Resources area: https://neurowaze.health
